reviews.title: Traveled During Pandemic
reviews.text: I would recommend this hotel only if the interested party wanted to be in West Beverly Hills close to the Beverly Grove shopping mall directly across the street,  or who didn't plan to stay in the room much.  The furniture in the Presidential suite could stand to be replaced especially the couch in room 1001.  The hotel is pet friendly and this couch had stains on it that appeared to be from an animal from the smell of it.  There were also stains in one of the chairs.  The cabinet doors on the front of the tv stand were dangling when we opened it up to see what was inside.   The small desk with additional outlets were all pulled away from the side of the wood, preventing any usage.  The electric fireplace on the outside sitting area did not work, it was disconnected.  The king size bed was very uncomfortable, I felt like I was sleeping on a bed of marshmallows.  The headboard  had hand prints all over it and the nightstand had someone’s dried contacts stuck on it.  Also hair in shower.

reviews.title: Not worth saving $100. Look elsewhere.
reviews.text: The shower ran only scalding hot water. Unable to use the shower with no time to move rooms before checking out. Frankly, All plumbing is worse for wear. Location is so-so, not good for walking. Local restaurants have closed up - hotel dining room seems only option for breakfast. . Sidewalks torn up, being repaired. Valet parking slow and too expensive -$45.

reviews.title: Good location but seriously what was wrong with that mattress!
reviews.text: Overall the hotel itself isn't that bad, but you'd expect more. My biggest issue was with the bed and you can imagine why that's very critical. I started having back pain from the second night, requested a replacement for the mattress and was told that they can't help. Instead they've provided an extra fluffy layer... which didn't help one bit, obviously. I had to spend a few days with increasing pain everyday and they couldn't help. One of the worst beds I've ever slept in.... Maybe my room bed had an issue. The staff should have been more helpful. Awful experience, never again.

reviews.title: Convenient to the airport and good restaurants
reviews.text: The drive from the airport to the hotel, on surface roads, along La Cienega Drive is straight forward, so is the drive to downtown, via 3rd Road or Beveley Drive.  The bedroom and bathroom are both on the small side, being around 30 sq m total.  There is also only one coffee shop which is neither inexpensive nor that good.  The redeeming grace is that there are several good restaurants, including Lawry and Morton, along La Cienega Drive a few minutes from the hotel.

reviews.title: Satisfactory but not up to par for a Sofitel
reviews.text: The rooms are clean and comfortable - making it cosy and relaxing. Bathroom has sufficient lighting and space. However, service though when you receive it is good - getting the service is a challenge as it takes a while even to be acknowledged. Very different to Sofitel properties in Asia - especially in Taiwan, Singapore and Bali where the guests are prioritised. It took me 8 minutes and looking around the lobby to find a staff to help me retrieve my luggage from the luggage room. It never happens when i stay at other Sofitel properties.

reviews.title: The refrigerator is a blemish.
reviews.text:
The minibar has an automatic payment system, so the refrigerator cannot be used at all. If that's the case, I think it would be better to just inform guests that a refrigerator is not provided.

The restaurant is small, but all the food is delicious. The staff is friendly.

Water is provided through a shared water dispenser. It seems more convenient than requesting it every time.

The valet fee is very high. It seems to be common in LA. 
There is an additional resort fee. It feels unreasonable since I didn't use the pool, gym, or housekeeping at all.

There's a large hospital nearby, so the sound of sirens can be very loud and frequent at night, which is uncomfortable (it feels so loud that you wonder if they could turn it off, even in the early morning).

reviews.title: Convenient location
reviews.text:
Right opposite a huge mall with Macy’s, Sephora & so on. The Hop On Hop Off bus stop is there too. 
Parking is valet only at $65.99 per 24 hours. They charge a nightly “urban fee” of $32 when you check out. 

Some eateries nearby too like Chipotle. And walking distance to Melrose Avenue with some shopping. Also walking distance to a laundromat & Hertz where we rented our car. Overall very convenient for first-timers to LA. Wasn’t too difficult to get to LAX too at about 7pm on a Friday.

reviews.title: Is a very comfortable and relaxing stay
reviews.text: Sofitel Los Angeles is more like a business hotel, it is of a 4 to 5-star standard.  The main lobby may be small but looks class.  Staff are polite and friendly and offer to upgrade us to their suite room.  The surrounding area of the hotel is clean and neat.  The room is of standard size, it will be too small for more than 3 person. Toilet is clean and toiletries and special items such as shower cap, cotton buds, nail file are provided.  Bed is extremely comfortable.  Beverly Center is just located opposite the hotel which house many branded stores like Prada, LV, Forever 21, Macy etc.  We love this hotel very much and thank you for all yours reviews for this hotel.

reviews.title: Great for a partying weekend in LA
reviews.text: From the doormen and valet to the front desk, we were treated with a welcoming touch I haven't seen in a while. The room was very clean, though the design of a window looking into the room from the shower was a bit awkward for us. Rooms are not the biggest, but not small either. The club downstairs can be quiet loud if you're looking for a quiet room. I would probably request a room facing inwards rather than on the street. Otherwise the location is great if you want a five minute drive to Beverly HIlls for morning coffee and food. For the price, it's a good value and would recommend.

reviews.title: Never again.
reviews.positives: The beds (particularly the goose down pillows) were great.
reviews.negatives: The number one issue I had was having to pay $71.50 to park my car in their garage for one night. There’s no parking in the area (next to Beverly Center in West LA), so you’re essentially forced to use their valet parking (they don’t offer self parking). I was shocked when I was told it was $71.50 (when I checked in). Also, the hotel back up against the Beverly Center mall - so you have a giant wall (of a multi-story parking garage) blocking your view. The pool was old and sad - and far removed from the center of the property. Though the beds were very comfortable, the rooms were drab and outdated - felt like a $150 room - but you’re paying for location.

I enjoyed my stay at Sofitel and found the team on the ground very helpful and friendly. They go out of their way to ensure you're comfortable.

The room itself (standard room) was spacious with all the expected amenities. It did feel a little outdated and there wasn't a lot of natural light coming in. While still quite acceptable, the room was the least favorite thing of my stay.

I'd consider staying here again, perhaps just in a different room category.

reviews.title: Lovely hotel, great location
reviews.text:
Accor Hotels never disappoint.   The staff is friendly, courteous and knowledgeable.  Unfortunately, we are in the middle of the COVID pandemic, so certain services are unavailable, which is the same at any hotel. There is no open restaurant, bar or room service at this time.  The minibar is empty, but there is a coffee station in the room.  No parking valet service is available.  These things are not the fault of Sofitel.  The guests and staff all take it in stride, remain cordial and considerate, and I felt a certain comrade-in-arms vibe among all.

Whatever amenities can be offered are.  

Love the bed.  Down duvet was crisp white and immaculate, which is a good thing, because there was no top sheet.    Super comfortable.

The location is perfect for business, medical  and pleasure travelers.   Getting around is easy, and I would especially recommend this hotel for tourists, as it is close to Farmers Market and the Grove, the Miracle Mile, CBS, incredible shopping and dining.   It’s right in the middle of the Cedars Sinai medical complex, and a straight shot west to the beach, Beverly Hills and Century City, or east to Hollywood and downtown.     If you are not from L.A., wander into Cedars Sinai.  You'll be impressed by the zillions of donor recognition plaques, buildings, etc. 

I look forward to staying here again when life returns to normal, but even if it isn’t, this is a nice spot to land when you’re in L.A. 

Highly recommend!

reviews.title: 여러가지로 만족스러운 호텔~^^
reviews.text:
이번 연휴동안 딸아이를 데리고 처음으로 방문한 미국.. 그전에는 혼자 다니던 터라 그렇게 호텔에 민감하지 않았지만 아기가 있어서 여러모로 신경쓸 부분이 많았는데....
관광지 뿐만 아니라 쇼핑센터, 음식점등 가족과 함께 방문하기에 좋은 장소들과 너무나 가까워서 좋았고.. 시내랑 너무 가깝지도 않아 조용한 시내 호텔의 느낌이었답니다.
미국 호텔들이 아주 고가를 제외하곤 그만그만한 것을 가만하면 상당히 깨끗하고 우수한 호텔 시설을 갖추고 있어 좋았답니다.
만약 가족을 동반한 LA 휴가 계획이있으시다면, 강력추천드릴수 있을 것 같습니다^^

reviews.title: Классный отель.
reviews.text: Мне очень понравился отель, все в нем хорошо: и расположение, и чистота в номерах, и наличие бесплатной воды в номере и в лобби отеля, и дружелюбный персонал, и ресторан с очень вкусной кухней. Мы даже в этом ресторане покупали завтраки, которые были платные для взрослого гостя (то есть меня) и бесплатные для ребенка до 12 лет (моей дочери 10 лет). Это было самым большим преимуществом для меня, потому что вкусно, недорого и не надо далеко от отеля уходить. Кроме того, по пятницам и субботам в ресторане отеля проводятся музыкальные и ди-джей вечера, отлично. Напротив отеля большой торговый центр, рядом много всего нужного: кафе, рестораны, магазины и т.д. Большое спасибо за гостеприимство.

reviews.title: Average and not a great location
reviews.positives: The room was a good size, and the beds were comfortable. We were sharing a two double bed suite, and there was enough space for 3 people to unpack, which was great. The bathroom was also spacious and it was great to have both a bath and shower on offer.
reviews.negatives: When we arrived, our room was not ready at the check in time; the staff member looking after us forgot to attend to us, and another staff helped us after we were seen sitting in the loffy for 45 minutes post our check in. We weren't compensated for the fact that our room was not available and the staff had the audacity to tell us that we could pay over $1000USD more for another room that was ready, despite the fact our room should have been ready. We also had delayed luggage, and we informed the Hotel reception our luggage was delayed by the airline, and to please notify us on arrival. Instead by pure chance, we were lucky to intercept the Hotel staff refusing to take accept our bags from the courier despite the forewarning. Over all the staff were not good.

reviews.title: Noisy and dated
reviews.text:
We stayed 11 nights! I really wanted to give a positive review as  everyone works at the hotel’re all super nice, the location is great, spacious rooms and comfortable beds. 
But.…The hotel’s dated and not well maintained, with bad decor ( really bad art at the lobby)… it's not very clean - hair in the bath,  loud music from the club on the ground floor till late late, I really don't mind the ambulance from the hospital nearby, but they have an open-air DJ at the club…. And bad breakfast!
But the most annoying thing was the mini bar, every single item is with the sensor and you get charged after 10second you lift the item! So the little fridge is full with no extra room for you even to keep a small yogurt or apple... Imagine you have a jet-lagged 6 years old!  
We won’t return.

reviews.title: Reasonable option for Beverly Hills
reviews.positives: They let me use the pool while I was waiting to check in.  It is usable in the winter, since it is heated, but it doesn't get any sun at this time of year.  The minibar in the room is equipped with sensors -- if you touch anything, you bought it -- but when I asked the staff about that they offered to (and promptly did) deliver an empty minifridge to the room at no extra charge.  The shower in the room has a built-in seat.  The elevator service is prompt and efficient; the lighting in the room is decent; and the openable window (on to a fake balcony) was a welcome alternative to the over-blowing aircon.
reviews.negatives: The first night featured turndown service, which I hadn't expected:  Someone came in while I was out and moved my stuff off the bed.  The second day there was no service at all, probably because I had a Privacy sign on the door until early afternoon ... but when I eventually did go out there was a cleaner working on the room next door, so you would think they could figure that out.  The room rates are rather high, albeit lower than other 4-star hotels in the area.

reviews.title: Sofitel is still a worthwhile and convenient hotel.
reviews.positives:
Staff very pleasant. Rooms very good. Pillows to die for. View of Hollywood Hills from our room. 
They have added a free drinking water machine on each floor by the elevator which is a nice touch and saves money on in room bottles and effort to bring water in from the outside. They give you a metal bottle to use for the water dispensers. Excellent breakfast.
reviews.negatives: The tops of the metal bottles are sealed - which is good. However my older hands lack strength to twist off the tiny top without help. The Sofitel has stopped providing hard copy newspapers which I used to love. Instead they offer an online service which is very annoying as you have to open an account and close it later to avoid charges. I am sure the LA Times is still available and they should offer it for daily fee or free.

reviews.title: The best and friendliest team in the world

A heartfelt thank you to the entire team!

reviews.title: Great hotel but not if you have a car
reviews.positives:
The service and staff were exceptional! The bed linens were fantastic. It was incredibly clean. The food at the hotel was very good. In walking distance to food and shopping. 
We made use of the gym daily which was well maintained. We took our dog and they offered a doggy bed. They were incredibly friendly to the dog.
reviews.negatives: Overnight parking in the surrounding area is severely limited. There are about 3 meters in the front, which are taken. The surrounding neighborhood is restricted to permits. The mall across the street doesn't allow overnight parking. So you risk getting towed in most areas. The hotel offers valet parking at $71 per night per vehicle. We had 2 vehicles for 3 nights so we spent about $430 on parking alone. I tried looking for any other parking lot even a mile away but once you work out the cost it's about the same plus inconvenient.
